assertions.description: fetch is the API through which No-Vary-Search-controlled cache hits are observed in the page.
Request supports the cache init member ('default' etc.); No-Vary-Search interacts with the HTTP disk cache layer that this surface controls.
caches (CacheStorage) is exposed; No-Vary-Search is also acceptable in service-worker Cache lookups in some implementations.
PerformanceResourceTiming entries expose transferSize/encodedBodySize, the surfaces by which cache hits surface in the page.
Headers.prototype.get is callable; the No-Vary-Search response header is read via the standard fetch Headers API.
URLSearchParams is iterable; No-Vary-Search semantics are defined over the URL's query parameter set.
assertions.test: (()=>{const u=new URLSearchParams('a=1&b=2');let n=0;for(const _ of u)n++;return n===2;})()
